evidence: In order to respond to the impact of new technologies on our businesses, we regularly consider, and from time to time implement, new initiatives and changes to our business models

the legal and regulatory landscape for certain new technologies, such as AI, is uncertain and evolving and our compliance obligations could increase our costs or limit how we may use these technologies

Regulations governing new technological developments, such as developments in artificial intelligence (AI), including generative AI and large language model tools, remain unsettled, and these developments may affect aspects of our existing business models
Technological developments, including developments in generative AI tools that can be used to create competing low-cost content and products
the availability of certain AI tools has facilitated the creation of infringing works based on the unauthorized use of our IP
the availability of copyright protection and other legal protections for IP generated by certain new technologies, such as generative AI, is uncertain
the continued development and maintenance of this program is costly and requires ongoing monitoring and updating as technologies change, including as a result of the proliferation of AI tools

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: excerpts contain only generic references to considering new initiatives; no named AI programs, dollar amounts, or dedicated AI investment described — score reflects disclosed absence of specificity rather than confirmed low investment

ai_narrative_concreteness: all AI references are framed as external threats or regulatory uncertainty rather than internal initiatives; no named Disney AI use cases or deployment details present in excerpts

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: risk-section material is present and discusses multiple company-specific AI risk mechanisms (IP infringement via AI tools, cybersecurity proliferation of AI, regulatory uncertainty affecting business models, generative AI competition) with causal pathways to revenue/operations, supporting score of 4; no quantification or named governance bodies found to justify score of 5
